title: Cirrus: container/rootless env. var. passthrough
body:
#### What type of PR is this?

/kind other

#### What this PR does / why we need it:

Resolve a long-standing TODO item (and bug fix apparently) relating to
passing important env. var. values into special testing contexts.
Namely container-based and rootless testing.  These changes mostly come
from the (now battle-tested) work by @edsantiago in the podman CI
scripts.  Some podman-CI specific variables have been stripped out, and
other buildah-specific simplifications made.

#### How to verify it

Assuming all CI tests pass, manually examine the output from containerized and rootless testing tasks.  Near the top of the output log, all current env. vars. and values will be printed.  Ensure this list is as expected for the given testing context.

#### Which issue(s) this PR fixes:

Fixes: https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/4838

#### Special notes for your reviewer:

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
[Many, many flakes in 'Containerized integration'](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/6733955584491520) but most/all of them seem like networking or dns flakes :thinking: 

Certainly doing container-in-container stuff is somewhat fragile.  It's probably a long-shot, but @edsantiago, have you ever seen anything like this before (networking tests flaking over multiple days) with our (outer) fedora image or (inner) alpine image?

N/B:  This PR is resulting in many more env. vars. passing into the containerized testing context.  Maybe it's possible the failures are caused by this (somehow).

```
[+1682s] not ok 258 bud test RUN with a privileged command
[+1682s] # (from function `die' in file ./helpers.bash, line 332,
[+1682s] #  from function `run_buildah' in file ./helpers.bash, line 319,
[+1682s] #  in test file ./bud.bats, line 3503)
[+1682s] #   `run_buildah build $WITH_POLICY_JSON -t ${target} -f $BUDFILES/run-privd/Dockerfile $BUDFILES/run-privd' failed
[+1682s] # /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/buildah/tests /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/buildah/tests
[+1682s] # # [checking for: docker.io/library/alpine]
[+1682s] # # [restoring from cache: /var/tmp/buildah-image-cache.1 / docker.io/library/alpine]
[+1682s] # Getting image source signatures
[+1682s] # Copying blob
...cut...
[+1682s] # STEP 2/2: RUN apk add nginx
[+1682s] # fetch http://dl-cdn.alpinelinux.org/alpine/v3.10/main/x86_64/APKINDEX.tar.gz
[+1682s] # fetch http://dl-cdn.alpinelinux.org/alpine/v3.10/community/x86_64/APKINDEX.tar.gz
[+1682s] # ERROR: http://dl-cdn.alpinelinux.org/alpine/v3.10/main: temporary error (try again later)
[+1682s] # WARNING: Ignoring APKINDEX.00740ba1.tar.gz: No such file or directory
[+1682s] # ERROR: http://dl-cdn.alpinelinux.org/alpine/v3.10/community: temporary error (try again later)
[+1682s] # WARNING: Ignoring APKINDEX.d8b2a6f4.tar.gz: No such file or directory
[+1682s] # ERROR: unsatisfiable constraints:
[+1682s] #   nginx (missing):
[+1682s] #     required by: world[nginx]
```
`apk` errors are pretty common but they're usually transient. You say these are persistent? Could it be a `*_PROXY` envariable? Can you check environment for new passthrough of `net` or `prox`?

> usually transient. You say these are persistent?

It appears like it.  Every re-run of the failed 'containerized integration' task (linked above) has the same/similar networking/dns errors (pasted above).  Fortunately this PR does add a `show_env_vars()` call right at test-time, so there is [a context-relevant selection of them to check](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/6733955584491520?logs=integration_test#L2-L237).  

Evenso, nothing jumps out at me, and we never set net or proxy type env. vars previously.
That said, these tests are building images INSIDE a container, which tends to be fairly fragile.  Maybe one/more of the buildah or environment vars. could be causing this?

* `BUILDAH_ISOLATION=chroot` (seems unlikely)
* `STORAGE_DRIVER=vfs` (also seems unlikely)
* `SYSTEMD_EXEC_PID=943` (likely wrong in this context, but probably harmless?)
* `JOURNAL_STREAM=8:20727` (not sure what/where this comes from)
* `GOPROXY=direct` (probably irrelevant)
* `GONOPROXY=` (probably irrelevant)

Oof, they all seem like really big guesses.  I'm perplexed :confounded:

Also, I have to imagine other tests reach out to the network for various bits.  Even in the posted failure case, we did actually download the base-images at some point.  Could the image we're using maybe have a bad/old list of packaging mirrors maybe (wild guess)?
Ref: Recent 'containerized integration' task (passed) on main, [printout of pass-through env. vars.](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5004165156438016?logs=integration_test#L9-L62)
Force-push: Rebased on the off-chance it (somehow) fixes the "containerized integration" failures.
A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.
force-push: Rebased after image updates, perhaps there's a fix for "containerized integration" test failures which have plagued this PR.

Getting back to this finally.  It's still very perplexing, esp. since the rootless tests (which rely on the new `passthrough_envars()`) works perfectly fine. These are the main failures that need addressing:

Possibly a flake, I don't remember seeing this previously:
```
[+3102s] not ok 410 build-with-no-new-privileges-test
[+3102s] # (from function `run_buildah' in file ./helpers.bash, line 309,
[+3102s] #  in test file ./bud.bats, line 6123)
[+3103s] #   `run_buildah build --security-opt no-new-privileges $WITH_POLICY_JSON ${TEST_SCRATCH_DIR}' failed
[+3103s] # /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/buildah/tests /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/buildah/tests
[+3103s] # # [checking for: docker.io/library/alpine]
[+3103s] # # [restoring from cache: /var/tmp/buildah-image-cache.1 / docker.io/library/alpine]
[+3103s] # Getting image source signatures
[+3103s] # Copying blob sha256:9d16cba9fb961d1aafec9542f2bf7cb64acfc55245f9e4eb5abecd4cdc38d749
[+3103s] # Copying config sha256:961769676411f082461f9ef46626dd7a2d1e2b2a38e6a44364bcbecf51e66dd4
[+3103s] # Writing manifest to image destination
[+3103s] # # /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/buildah/tests/./../bin/buildah build --security-opt no-new-privileges --signature-policy /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/buildah/tests/./policy.json /var/tmp/buildah_tests.b5vvpg
[+3103s] # STEP 1/2: FROM alpine
[+3103s] # [ rc=124 (** EXPECTED 0 **) ]
[+3103s] # *** TIMED OUT ***
[+3103s] # /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/buildah/tests
```

Several tests fail with (this is the most perplexing):
```
[+4906s] # STEP 2/3: RUN apk add openssh-client
[+4906s] # fetch http://dl-cdn.alpinelinux.org/alpine/v3.10/main/x86_64/APKINDEX.tar.gz
[+4906s] # ERROR: http://dl-cdn.alpinelinux.org/alpine/v3.10/main: temporary error (try again later)
```

I'm guessing it's not possible to use `--network=private` inside a container that has `--net="container:registry"` set on it.  This probably needs to be skipped:
```
[+4777s] not ok 729 run --network should override build --network
[+4777s] # (from function `die' in file ./helpers.bash, line 332,
[+4777s] #  from function `run_buildah' in file ./helpers.bash, line 319,
[+4777s] #  in test file ./run.bats, line 821)
[+4777s] #   `run_buildah run --network=private $cid wget google.com' failed
[+4777s] # /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/buildah/tests /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/buildah/tests
[+4777s] # # /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/buildah/tests/./../bin/buildah from --network=none --quiet --pull=false --signature-policy /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/buildah/tests/./policy.json alpine
[+4777s] # alpine-working-container
[+4777s] # # /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/buildah/tests/./../bin/buildah run alpine-working-container wget google.com
[+4777s] # wget: bad address 'google.com'
```

The root call to run the tests inside a container comes from `lib.sh` function `in_podman()`.  The whole command (line-wrapped for readability) is:
```
podman run -i --name=in_podman_6040450889416704 --net=container:registry --privileged \
--cgroupns=host -e ALPINE_FQIN -e CI -e CIRRUS_ARCH -e CIRRUS_BASE_BRANCH -e CIRRUS_BASE_SHA \
-e CIRRUS_BRANCH -e CIRRUS_BUILD_ID -e CIRRUS_BUILD_SOURCE -e CIRRUS_CHANGE_IN_REPO \
-e CIRRUS_CHANGE_MESSAGE -e CIRRUS_CHANGE_TIMESTAMP -e CIRRUS_CHANGE_TITLE -e CIRRUS_CI \
-e CIRRUS_CLONE_DEPTH -e CIRRUS_COMMIT_MESSAGE -e CIRRUS_CPU -e CIRRUS_DEFAULT_BRANCH \
-e CIRRUS_ENV -e CIRRUS_HTTP_CACHE_HOST -e CIRRUS_LAST_GREEN_BUILD_ID \
-e CIRRUS_LAST_GREEN_CHANGE -e CIRRUS_OS -e CIRRUS_PR -e CIRRUS_PR_BODY \
-e CIRRUS_PR_DRAFT -e CIRRUS_PR_LABELS -e CIRRUS_PR_TITLE -e CIRRUS_REPO_CLONE_HOST \
-e CIRRUS_REPO_CLONE_URL -e CIRRUS_REPO_FULL_NAME -e CIRRUS_REPO_ID -e CIRRUS_REPO_NAME \
-e CIRRUS_REPO_OWNER -e CIRRUS_RESOLUTION_STRATEGY -e CIRRUS_SHELL -e CIRRUS_TASK_ID \
-e CIRRUS_TASK_NAME -e CIRRUS_TASK_NAME_ALIAS -e CIRRUS_USER_COLLABORATOR \
-e CIRRUS_USER_PERMISSION -e CIRRUS_WORKING_DIR -e CI_NODE_INDEX -e CI_NODE_TOTAL \
-e DEBIAN_CACHE_IMAGE_NAME -e DEBIAN_NAME -e DEST_BRANCH -e FEDORA_CACHE_IMAGE_NAME \
-e FEDORA_NAME -e GOPATH -e GOSRC -e IN_PODMAN_IMAGE -e IN_PODMAN_NAME \
-e PRIOR_FEDORA_CACHE_IMAGE_NAME -e PRIOR_FEDORA_NAME -e PRIV_NAME -e REGISTRY_FQIN \
-e ROOTLESS_USER -e SCRIPT_BASE -e IN_PODMAN=false -e CONTAINER=podman \
-e CGROUP_MANAGER=cgroupfs -v /root/auth:/root/auth:ro -v /sys/fs/cgroup:/sys/fs/cgroup:rw \
-v /dev/fuse:/dev/fuse:rw \
-v /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/buildah:/var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/buildah:z \
--workdir /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/buildah --rm \
in_podman_6040450889416704:latest ./contrib/cirrus/test.sh integration
```

So it's all networking and/or DNS related failures.  The strangest by far are the `apk add <whatever>` failures.  At this point, I'm completely stumped.  It's been a while, but IIRC the problem does reproduce using `get_ci_vm.sh`.

@edsantiago @nalind @rhatdan any other ideas of things to check or try?

The top-level `podman ... --net="container:registry"` couldn't be the problem, right?  That should still allow internet connectivity, eh?  :thinking: 

Edit: [Link to logs](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/6096021122449408).
Force-push: The containerized tests are passing with the addition of BUILDAH_ISOLATION=chroot and STORAGE_DRIVER=vfs, but they need to be excluded in the case of the rootless integration tests.
Whew! That took some doing, thanks. LGTM but I'd like one more set of eyeballs before merge.

> Whew! That took some doing, thanks. LGTM but I'd like one more set of eyeballs before merge.

As was made very clear yesterday, another pair of eyeballs on a tricky PR are worth their weight in gold (not that gold nuggets would provide very robust vision) :rofl: 

Thanks again @edsantiago

The tests needed to be restarted due to changes in the repo.  Once they pass:
LGTM
